column7: Specifies whether to create a byte enable for port A and B. Turn on these options if you want to mask the input data so that only specific bytes, nibbles, or bits of data are written.

column4: Create byte enable for port B
column7: Specifies whether to create a byte enable for port A and B. The potion to create a byte enable for port B is only available when you select two read/write ports.

Specifies whether to enable the ECC that performs single-error correction, double-adjacent-error correction and triple-adjacent-error correction. Non-adjacent double/tripple error correction/detection is not guaranteed. Adjacent/non-adjacent 4-bits errors detection is not guaranteed.

Specifies whether to enable the ECC that performs single-error correction, double-adjacent-error correction and triple-adjacent-error correction. Non-adjacent double/tripple error correction/detection is not guaranteed. Adjacent/non-adjacent 4-bits errors detection is not guaranteed.
Specifies whether to enable  ECC Pipeline Registers before the output decoder to achieve that same performance as non-ECC mode at the expense of one cycle of latency.
Specifies whether to enable the ECC Encoder Bypass which allows user to selectively insert parity bits into the memory through eccencparity port.

Specifies whether to enable the Coherent Read  to present user with coherent memory read so that user can read out current memory content, perform operation on top of the content, and write back to the same location in the same cycle.

Specifies whether to enable the Force To Zero. Force To Zero can help to improve performance of glue logic when memory depth selected is larger than a single memory block. When Force To Zero is enabled, output data will be set to '0' when read enable is deasserted.
